content:
Good morning,

this usually happens due to an IMAP synchronization issue between Thunderbird and your mail server. The folders are normally not actually deleted but incorrectly shown under trash after a sync error or folder index problem. check your account via webmail first to see if the folders are still there, as in many cases they can simply be moved back from trash on the server side You can also try re-subscribing to folders in Thunderbird and restarting the client to force a fresh sync. If the issue is still present, removing and re-adding the account in Thunderbird often restores the correct folder structure
creator: yulyabrylk

content: Komen ze toevallig in Ongewenst terecht? Gaat dit om een IMAP-account, zo ja, probeer de account eens te verwijderen en opnieuw aan te maken.

content: De mail is ook via een zoekopdracht niet terug te vinden, in geen enkele map. Daar komt bij dat de weinige mail die nog in Postvak In staat ook niet te openen is, als je er op klikt is de inhoud leeg. Account verwijderen en opnieuw toevoegen is ook al geprobeerd, zonder resultaat.

content: Nieuw profiel aangemaakt, maar ook dit heeft geen resultaat. Bij aanmelding komt nog een melding: berichtkop 1 van 72 naar Postvak IN downloaden. Het lijkt erop dat het hierop blijft hangen, het downloaden gebeurt niet.
creator: h.lautenbach

content:
2 ideeën:

  1. er staat op de server een corrupt bericht dat niet kan worden gedownload; probeer via webmail bijvoorbeeld de eerste 10 berichten te verplaatsen naar een andere (tijdelijke) map;
  2. de virusscanner ligt dwars, schakel deze eens tijdelijk uit.
creator: Mozilla-assistent

content: Het corrupte bericht is na lang zoeken, via een omweg hoogstwaarschijnlijk gevonden. Het gaat om een bericht van 3 mei, 17.15 uur, met foutmelding LE338 NO (serverlog),zie bijgevoegde afbeelding. Betreffende mail is echter niet te vinden in Thunderbird en daardoor door mij op geen enkele manier te verwijderen.
creator: h.lautenbach

content: Probeer dit bericht via webmail te verwijderen.
creator: Mozilla-assistent

content: Verwijderen via webmail was ook niet mogelijk. Vervolgens contact opgenomen met de provider, die heeft de betreffende mail verwijderd. Het bleek te gaan om een mailbericht van 0 byte, iets wat niet zou moeten kunnen. Het probleem is hiermee opgelost, alles werkt weer zoals het hoort.
creator: h.lautenbach

content: Goed om te horen dat het is opgelost en Thunderbird weer werkt. Dank ook voor de terugkoppeling.
creator: Mozilla-assistent

content:
Thunderbird 148+ on Windows 11

Why don't you use the latest Release version of Thunderbird? https://www.thunderbird.net/thunderbird/all/

The account only offers “NTLM” or “Normal password” authentication methods. OAuth2 is not available in the account settings.

See https://support.mozilla.org/kb/thunderbird-and-exchange for setup instructions.

content:
I wish to continue with Thunderbird.

That's fine. All you need to do is setup your account for Yahoo in Thunderbird, using your existing email address. Before starting, make sure cookies are allowed in Thunderbird.

In the main Thunderbird window press Alt or F10 to bring up the menu bar in case it is hidden. Then, File (Alt-F) - New - Email Account Provide your credentials and follow the instructions of the wizard.

Also see https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/yahoo-email-migration-overview https://help.yahoo.com/kb/new-yahoo-mail/SLN36803.html
creator: christ1

content: When Norton VPN does not allow the POP protocol, then there is nothing Thunderbird can do. You'd have to check with Norton. I'm wondering why you'd need a VPN to retrieve email in the first place.

content: crist1, Thank you for your clear reply! I did find that NortonVPN does allow per-App by-passes, and this works!

content:
Got to love developers that think they are making things easier for us.

Lets first try and work out which is which.

So lets check a couple of things are correct before deleting anything. Right click your account name and select subscribe. Are all three folders shown (I expect they will be.) Open account settings. From that same right click menu select settings Under the "Server Settings" pane check the folder that is named as the correct one for trash. Change it is necessary. While we are cleaning up. Your image shows two sent folders. So under copies and folders, check the folder name actually used for sent mail by selecting other and setting it manually to "sent mail" on the account name (that is what Google use). I think what is happening is Thunderbird is using sent and Google is using sent mail and both are being displayed as "sent".

So after the change you should still have two sent folders, but one will no longer have the green icon and should be deleted.

Note that you can right click on a folder name and select properties from the menu. This will show the "real" name of the folder not what Thunderbird is using to display to you. It is this substitution that evoked my initial comment.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
At this point Thunderbird does not offer an email address, so you would have to continue to have a yahoo account that Thunderbird could use to collect your mail from. Or you could elect to create an account with another provider to have Thunderbird collect mail from.

As fort a demonstration. Install it. it is not as if it does anything to your existing account other than get your mails and sync the content with your existing account. Otherwise the best I can suggest is you have a look at some of the examples on the website. As Thunderbird is free and very customizable along with the fact that installing it is non destructive, I don't think anyone has ever seriously considered any sort of demonstration. It is more a try it and like it type thing.
